data.title: Bitmine Immersion's Tom Lee-led SPAC Futurecrest prices ~$250M IPO
data.description:
Futurecrest Acquisition (FCRS) announced on Friday the pricing of an IPO of 25M units at $10.00 per unit to raise proceeds of ~$250M.

The blank check company's management team is led by Bitmine Immersion Technologies (NYSE:BMNR) chair Thomas J. Lee.

BMNR has been on a gaining spree since investors Peter Thiel and Cathie Wood's ARK Invest disclosed interest in the Bitcoin and Ethereum network company.

While the company said it may pursue an acquisition opportunity in any business or industry, but it expects to focus on a target in the AI, digital assets, fintech, infrastructure, robotics, and communications industries.

The special purpose acquisition company had filed a preliminary prospectus with the Securities and Exchange Commission for the initial public offering on September 17.

The units are expected to be listed on the NYSE and begin trading on September 26 under the ticker symbol FCRS.U.

Each unit consists of one class A share and one-quarter of one redeemable warrant. The warrant entitles the holder to purchase one class A share at $11.50 per share.

The company said an amount equal to $10.00 per unit will be deposited into a trust account upon the closing of the offering.

Once the securities constituting the units begin separate trading, the class A shares and warrants are expected to be listed on the NYSE under the symbols FCRS and FCRS WS.

The offering is expected to close on September 29.

Additionally, the underwriters are granted a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 3.75M units at the IPO price to cover over-allotments, if any.

data.title: Boeing secures sizable orders from Turkish Airlines, Norwegian Air
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) +0.7% pre-market Friday after announcing aircraft orders from Turkish Airlines and Norwegian Air Shuttle, as the planemaker attempts to reset its business.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) said Turkish Airlines placed an order for as many as 75 787 Dreamliners, its largest ever purchase of Boeing widebody aircraft, comprised of 35 787-9 models, 15 of the larger 787-10 model and options for 25 787 Dreamliners; deliveries are scheduled between 2029 and 2034.

Negotiations are ongoing with Rolls-Royce (OTCPK:RYCEY) (OTCPK:RYCEF) and GE Aerospace (GE) regarding engines, spare engines and engine maintenance services for the planes, the airline said.

Boeing (BA) also said Turkish Airlines plans to buy up to 150 737 MAX aircraft in what would be its largest Boeing single-aisle order; the airline said it is in discussions with engine manufacturer CFM International - the 50/50 joint venture between GE Aerospace (GE) and France's Safran Aircraft Engines (OTCPK:SAFRF) (OTCPK:SAFRY) - and would place the 737 MAX order if the talks are successful.

The deal has long been in the making, as the airline's chairman revealed a planned purchase of 225 aircraft a year ago; the latest announcement came after President Trump met Turkey's President Erdogan in Washington.

Financial terms were not disclosed, but buyers typically receive steep discounts for large aircraft orders.

Also, Boeing (BA) said Norwegian Air Shuttle exercised an option to buy an additional 30 737 MAX 8 aircraft, bringing its total firm order to 80 aircraft in a deal reached in 2022; the airline confirmed the final aircraft delivery is now scheduled for 2031, following adjustments to the delivery timeline.

data.title: Xiaomi plans next-gen chip for phones, but won't have one annually like Apple: report
data.description:
Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) (OTCPK:XIACY) is planning a new high-end chip for its smartphones but won’t release one on an annual basis like Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), CNBC reported, citing a company executive.

Xu Fei, vice president at Xiaomi, discussed the Chinese company's semiconductor ambitions, and provided details about its roadmap for the product, the report added.

Xiaomi did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Xu said that the company was “planning ahead” for its next generation of chip, but said she can’t promise it will release a new SoC each year.

“We are a newcomer here, we need to learn and we need to plan,” said Xu.

In May, Xiaomi's Founder and CEO Lei Jun said that the company had started mass production of its in-house developed advanced mobile chip, Xring O1. In June it was reported that the company may have started development of the new XRING 02 chip, with a leak revealing that the trademark for the silicon has already been filed.

Xu told the media outlet that 1 million units of the XRing 01 will be shipped but Xiaomi would need to make 10 million units per chip release for it to be a breakeven part of the business, the report added.

“So for us, we know we probably need to have 10 years patience for the SoC to finally break even,” said Xu, according to the report. “So at the first time, we just need to make sure the experience is good enough, the performance is good enough.”

The Xiaomi 17 smartphone, which was launched this week, features Qualcomm’s latest System on a Chip, or SoC.

“For Qualcomm, MediaTek, they are super, extremely good partners. We’ve been working them for 15 years, so we will continue this path. And at the same time, we’ll select ... [the] right product to try our own chipset. We are going with two solutions at the same time,”said Xu.

Xu added that the company has made it clear to its partners "don’t be too worried at all," the report added.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) first unveiled its own chip in 2010 under the A series. It has released a new A series chip each year, with the latest one, known as the A19 and A19 Pro, seen in the iPhone 17 models.

data.title: NETSTREIT secures $450M in additional financing
data.description:
NETSTREIT (NYSE:NTST) has secured $450 million in additional financing and amended its existing credit facilities with PNC Bank (PNC), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Truist Bank (TFC).

The new term loan credit agreement, agented by PNC Bank, National Association, provides for a $200 million, 5.5-year loan (the 2031 Term Loan), fully funded on the closing date, and a $250 million, 7-year loan (the 2032 Term Loan), partially funded with $100 million; $150 million available until September 25, 2026.

The 2031 loan is fully hedged with an all-in interest rate, as of October 1, 2025, of 4.59% per annum, while $200 million of the 2032 loan is hedged at 4.92%, with the remaining $50 million currently unhedged. Interest is payable monthly or at the end of the applicable interest period in arrears on any outstanding borrowings.

Each of the PNC, Wells Fargo, and Truist credit agreements were amended to, among other changes necessary to conform to the PNC term loan agreement, remove the SOFR credit spread adjustment.

data.title: Firefly Aerospace is a catalyst call idea at Deutsche Bank on the expectation for a near-term rally
data.description:
Deutsche Bank issued a Catalyst Call Buy Idea on Firefly Aerospace (NASDAQ:FLY) on the expectation of a near-term share price rally.

Analyst Edison Yu noted that Firefly (NASDAQ:FLY) shares have sold off recently, which he thinks was likely exacerbated by recent negative headlines. "We believe such comments are not accurate based on prior analysis, and management will have a chance to prove us right in the coming 2-3 weeks with the launch of Alpha FLAT007," he advised.

Yu and his team think a share price bounce would initially be triggered by an announcement of the launch date/window and signal technical readiness, which is seen as likely to boost market confidence. The key near-term catalyst is the expected announcement of the FLTA007 launch date, which the firm sees occurring within the next two to three weeks.

"We would expect the stock to respond positively heading into the actual launch countdown, with a positive bias for the actual launch considering the corrective actions taken following the anomaly on FLTA006," wrote Yu.

Firefly Alpha FLAT007 will be the seventh launch of the company's Alpha rocket. The mission will carry a tactical satellite (TacSat-Demo) for Lockheed Martin (LMT). The payload is a tactical satellite, intended to showcase new technology. The mission is the second under a multi-launch agreement for up to 25 missions between Firefly and Lockheed Martin (LMT).

Deutsche Bank assigned a price target of $45 to Firefly Aerospace (FLY).

Shares of Firefly Aerospace (FLY) traded flat at $37.65 in the premarket after a 7.2% decline on Thursday. The IPO was priced at $45 per share in early August.

data.title: Regeneron nabs FDA label expansion for Ultragenyx-partnered cholesterol drug
data.description:
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN) announced on Friday that the U.S. FDA approved a label expansion for its anti-cholesterol agent Evkeeza, marketed globally with Ultragenyx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:RARE).

Accordingly, the injectable, first approved in 2021 for adults and adolescents with homozygous familial hypercholesterolemia ((HoFH)), will also be available for U.S. children aged 1–5 years with the rare disorder when added to diet and exercise and other lipid-lowering agents.

The approval was based on data from six children who received Evkeeza under the U.S. expanded access program or the ex-U.S. compassionate use program, the New York-based biotech said.

HoFH affects about 1,300 Americans, causing them to develop cardiac and arterial diseases even during their early years of life due to the accumulation of LDL-C, also known as bad cholesterol. In 2022, Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) partnered with Ultragenyx (NASDAQ:RARE) to develop and commercialize Evkeeza in overseas markets.

data.title: Strong or weak: What's really going on with the U.S. economy?
data.description:
There have been many mixed signals in recent months, complicating the economic picture for investors, Fed policymakers, and everyone in between. A positive earnings season and the AI boom have boosted markets, consumer spending is resilient, and GDP growth is looking stronger than previously thought. On the other hand, the labor market slowdown is real, while inflation has remained above the central bank's 2% target for nearly five years now, and sweeping tariffs could continue to push that figure higher.

SA commentary: "It is becoming apparent the U.S. economy is being supported by very few pillars right now," Investing Group Leader Bret Jensen writes in If The U.S. Economy Is So Good. "The first is the federal government running a fiscal deficit of 6%-7% of GDP in recent years. With interest needed to service an over $37 trillion obligation, obviously this is unsustainable at some point."

"Then we have huge investments in building AI data centers being made by tech giants like Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOG). Will they pay off in the years ahead and produce large amounts of return on investment, or will some of these investments turn out like the tens of billions Meta Platforms (META) sunk into building out the Metaverse?"

"Finally, one other thing driving the economy is the small slice of the American population who saw price appreciation in their assets (stocks, real estate, etc...) outpace the rising cost of living over the past five years. The top 10% of income earners (roughly $250,000 of annual income or higher) are now responsible for nearly half of all consumer spending. They also own 87% of all equity value. If housing values continue to decline and/or stocks start to falter, this key source of consumer spending could weaken."

Next up: The Fed's favorite inflation gauge will be published at 8:30 AM ET, providing the latest glimpse into the state of the U.S. economy. Federal Reserve officials will be keyed in to the core personal consumption expenditures price index, which they consider the best measure of underlying inflation trends. Pressures are likely to have persisted in August, but also keep an eye on other important parts of the Personal Income and Outlays report, like consumer spending and income growth.

data.title: Fractyl Health stock gains after pricing of $60M underwritten offering
data.description:
• Fractyl Health (NASDAQ:GUTS) on Friday announced the pricing of an underwritten offering of 60 million shares of its common stock at a price of $1.00 apiece, fueling gains in premarket trading.
  • All the securities are being offered by Fractyl.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering to Fractyl are expected to be approximately $60 million, before deducting underwriting discounts and commissions and other offering expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about September 29, 2025.
  • Fractyl Health (NASDAQ:GUTS) stock up 28% premarket at $1.31.

data.title: U.S. auto sales are forecast by J.D. Power to rise 4.5% in September amid the temporary EV demand spike
data.description:
J.D. Power estimated that total new-vehicle sales for September 2025, including retail, will increase 0.1% year-over-year to 1,232,200 vehicles after adjusting for the extra selling day this September compared to a year ago. On an unadjusted basis, sales volume is expected to be up 4.5% from 2024.

The seasonally adjusted annualized rate for total new-vehicle sales is expected to be 16.2 million units, up 0.3 million units from September 2024.

"In aggregate, September sales results point to another month of strong demand for new vehicles. However, as has been the case for the past few months, assessing the health of the industry requires a closer look at the underlying market dynamics," highlighted Thomas King, president of the data and analytics division at J.D. Power. He noted that the biggest driver of September's strong sales pace is temporarily inflated demand for electric vehicles due to the federal EV tax credit expiring on September 30. EV share of retail sales is expected to reach a record of 12.2% this month vs. 9.6% a year ago.

The average new-vehicle retail transaction price in September is expected to reach $45,795, up 2.9% from September 2024. The average manufacturer incentive per vehicle is on track to reach $3,116, an increase of just $24 from August and a decrease of $3 from a year ago. Expressed as a percentage of MSRP, incentive spending is at 6.1%, a decrease of 0.2 percentage points from a year ago.

Trucks/SUVs are on pace to account for 82.1% of new-vehicle retail sales vs. 80.5% a year ago. 

The average interest rate for new-vehicle loans in September is 6.51%, a decrease of 25 basis points from a year ago, according to J.D. Power.

Looking ahead, a very significant decline in EV sales is expected by J.D. Power for October, reflecting both the effect of the federal EV tax credit expiring and the pull-forward effect of all the electric vehicle purchases that were accelerated into the summer period.

Auto stocks: Toyota (TM), Tesla (TSLA), General Motors (NYSE:GM), Honda (HMC), Ferrari (RACE), Ford (NYSE:F), Hyundai (HYMTF), Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY), Daimler (DDAIF), Volkswagen (VWAY), Subaru (OTCPK:FUJHY), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), and Lucid Group (LCID).

data.title: Chijet Motor stock surges on offering to raise $20M
data.description:
• Chijet Motor Company (NASDAQ:CJET) announced a private placement with non-U.S. institutional investors to raise up to $20 million by selling units of one ordinary share at $0.15 plus three warrants.
  • Each warrant can be used to buy one share at $0.18 within three years, with some cashless exercise options.
  • CJET +39.2% premarket to $0.23.
  • Source: Press Release
